clean_output:
article_id: art-2026-03-08-016
summary: A reported orchestrator-plus-sub-agent coding workflow appears to have reduced main-thread context load in one rewrite task, but the success claim remains tightly bounded by thin verification.
review_confidence: high
mechanisms:
  - claim_text: Sub-agent delegation reduced main-thread context burden by keeping detailed subtask work outside the orchestrator thread.
    evidence_ids:
      - evidence-02
      - evidence-12
      - evidence-01
    mechanism_text: The source explicitly says delegation was used to avoid hitting the model context window limit, with sub-agents handling local work while the main thread stayed focused on coordination, verification, and integration.
    bounds: This mechanism is supported for this reported experiment only and does not establish that the same pattern will scale or perform reliably across other coding tasks.
    confidence: medium
bounded_claims:
  - claim_text: The workflow used a main orchestrator dialogue that delegated tasks to sub-agents, then verified and integrated their outputs during a LiteLLM provider rewrite.
    evidence_ids:
      - evidence-01
      - evidence-03
    bounds: The evidence supports the reported workflow structure and task scope in this case, but not any broader claim that this structure is generally superior.
  - claim_text: The experiment completed in about four hours of agent time with about half an hour of human effort, 16 sub-agent sessions, and a total spend of $13.86.
    evidence_ids:
      - evidence-04
      - evidence-06
      - evidence-07
      - evidence-11
    bounds: These are reported case metrics for one run; they do not show which parts of the workflow caused the efficiency or whether similar results would repeat elsewhere.
  - claim_text: The app appears to have worked in this case, but verification coverage was limited.
    evidence_ids:
      - evidence-09
      - evidence-10

      - evidence-11
    bounds: These are reported case metrics for one run; they do not show which parts of the workflow caused the efficiency or whether similar results would repeat elsewhere.
  - claim_text: The app appears to have worked in this case, but verification coverage was limited.
    evidence_ids:
      - evidence-09
      - evidence-10
    bounds: The source supports only a bounded functionality claim because observed operation and five green tests are too thin to establish broad correctness, robustness, or reliability.
